posts.name: Yihui Lai
posts.text:
Dear Combine Experts,
I am encountering a buffer-related issue while performing a combination for a spin-1 search:
Error in TBufferFile::CheckByteCount: object of class RooWorkspace read too many bytes: 1141945592 instead of 68203768

Upon investigation, I found that the problem originates from data_obs growing rapidly when combining analyses with different observables.
Reproducing the Issue
The datacard and setup details are documented here:
CMS Analysis Datacards
To demonstrate the issue, consider the following combination of three datacards:
combineCards.py input/shapes_DY_Vp_VV_VH_jjjj/datacard_DY_combined_3000.txt input/shapes_DY_Zp_tt_0l/datacard_DY_combined_3000.txt input/shapes_DY_Zp_ll/datacard_DY_Zp_ll_3000.txt > combined.txt
text2workspace.py input/shapes_DY_Vp_VV_VH_jjjj/datacard_DY_combined_3000.txt --out workspace_VV.root --for-fits --no-wrappers --optimize-simpdf-constraints cms --X-pack-asympows --X-optimizeMHDependency=fixed --optimize-simpdf-constraints=cms --use-histsum  --use-histsum --mass 125.38
text2workspace.py input/shapes_DY_Zp_tt_0l/datacard_DY_combined_3000.txt --out workspace_tt.root --for-fits --no-wrappers --optimize-simpdf-constraints cms --X-pack-asympows --X-optimizeMHDependency=fixed --optimize-simpdf-constraints=cms --use-histsum  --use-histsum --mass 125.38
text2workspace.py input/shapes_DY_Zp_ll/datacard_DY_Zp_ll_3000.txt --out workspace_ll.root --for-fits --no-wrappers --optimize-simpdf-constraints cms --X-pack-asympows --X-optimizeMHDependency=fixed --optimize-simpdf-constraints=cms --use-histsum  --use-histsum --mass 125.38
text2workspace.py combined.txt --out workspace_combined.root --for-fits --no-wrappers --optimize-simpdf-constraints cms --X-pack-asympows --X-optimizeMHDependency=fixed --optimize-simpdf-constraints=cms --use-histsum  --use-histsum --mass 125.38

The observables are different in these analyses:
Singularity> root -l workspace_combined.root
root [2] w->data("data_obs")->Print()
RooDataSet::data_obs[MJ1,MJ2,MJJ,jetmass_LOW_default,resmass_default,jetmass_SIG_default,jetmass_HIGH_default,mass_dielectron_2016_EBEB,blindState,mass_dielectron_2016_EBEE,mass_dielectron_Legacy2017_EBEB,mass_dielectron_Legacy2017_EBEE,mass_dielectron_Legacy2018_EBEB,mass_dielectron_Legacy2018_EBEE,mass_dimuon_Moriond2017_BB,mass_dimuon_Moriond2017_BE,mass_dimuon_Legacy2017_BB,mass_dimuon_Legacy2017_BE,mass_dimuon_Legacy2018_BB,mass_dimuon_Legacy2018_BE,CMS_channel,weight:_weight_] = 691192 entries (222225 weighted)

Checking data_obs Size Growth
To inspect the growth of data_obs, I used the following script:
import ROOT
import sys

ws_list = ["workspace_VV.root", "workspace_tt.root", "workspace_ll.root", "workspace_combined.root"]

for ws in ws_list:
f=ROOT.TFile(ws,"READ")
w=f.Get("w")
print("checking {}".format(ws))
for obj in w.allData():
print("---> {} is {}, numEntries: {}, getSize of each entry {}, assume 8 bytes, total size is {}*{}={} MB".format(obj.GetName(), obj.ClassName(), obj.numEntries(), obj.get().getSize(), obj.numEntries(), obj.get().getSize(),obj.numEntries()* obj.get().getSize()*8/1024.0/1024.0 ))

Observed Output
Singularity> python print.py
checking workspace_VV.root
---> data_obs is RooDataSet, numEntries: 688000, getSize of each entry 4, assume 8 bytes, total size is 688000*4*8/1024.0/1024.0=20.99609375 MB
checking workspace_tt.root
---> data_obs is RooDataSet, numEntries: 1980, getSize of each entry 5, assume 8 bytes, total size is 1980*5*8/1024.0/1024.0=0.0755310058594 MB
checking workspace_ll.root
---> data_obs is RooDataSet, numEntries: 1212, getSize of each entry 14, assume 8 bytes, total size is 1212*14*8/1024.0/1024.0=0.129455566406 MB
checking workspace_combined.root
---> data_obs is RooDataSet, numEntries: 691192, getSize of each entry 21, assume 8 bytes, total size is 691192*21*8/1024.0/1024.0=110.740905762 MB

Issue Summary
Instead of growing linearly, data_obs expands significantly during the combination, likely due to the introduction of additional observable dimensions. This rapid increase ultimately exceeds the ROOT 1 GB limit per object, causing the error.
Besides aligning the name of fitting observable in the workspace, I wonder would you have any recommendations on how to handle this?
Best regards,
Yihui
posts.cooked_html:
Dear Combine Experts,

I am encountering a buffer-related issue while performing a combination for a spin-1 search:

Error in TBufferFile::CheckByteCount: object of class RooWorkspace read too many bytes: 1141945592 instead of 68203768

Upon investigation, I found that the problem originates from data_obs growing rapidly when combining analyses with different observables.

Reproducing the Issue

The datacard and setup details are documented here:
CMS Analysis Datacards

To demonstrate the issue, consider the following combination of three datacards:

combineCards.py input/shapes_DY_Vp_VV_VH_jjjj/datacard_DY_combined_3000.txt input/shapes_DY_Zp_tt_0l/datacard_DY_combined_3000.txt input/shapes_DY_Zp_ll/datacard_DY_Zp_ll_3000.txt > combined.txt
text2workspace.py input/shapes_DY_Vp_VV_VH_jjjj/datacard_DY_combined_3000.txt --out workspace_VV.root --for-fits --no-wrappers --optimize-simpdf-constraints cms --X-pack-asympows --X-optimizeMHDependency=fixed --optimize-simpdf-constraints=cms --use-histsum  --use-histsum --mass 125.38
text2workspace.py input/shapes_DY_Zp_tt_0l/datacard_DY_combined_3000.txt --out workspace_tt.root --for-fits --no-wrappers --optimize-simpdf-constraints cms --X-pack-asympows --X-optimizeMHDependency=fixed --optimize-simpdf-constraints=cms --use-histsum  --use-histsum --mass 125.38
text2workspace.py input/shapes_DY_Zp_ll/datacard_DY_Zp_ll_3000.txt --out workspace_ll.root --for-fits --no-wrappers --optimize-simpdf-constraints cms --X-pack-asympows --X-optimizeMHDependency=fixed --optimize-simpdf-constraints=cms --use-histsum  --use-histsum --mass 125.38
text2workspace.py combined.txt --out workspace_combined.root --for-fits --no-wrappers --optimize-simpdf-constraints cms --X-pack-asympows --X-optimizeMHDependency=fixed --optimize-simpdf-constraints=cms --use-histsum  --use-histsum --mass 125.38

The observables are different in these analyses:

Singularity> root -l workspace_combined.root
root [2] w->data("data_obs")->Print()
RooDataSet::data_obs[MJ1,MJ2,MJJ,jetmass_LOW_default,resmass_default,jetmass_SIG_default,jetmass_HIGH_default,mass_dielectron_2016_EBEB,blindState,mass_dielectron_2016_EBEE,mass_dielectron_Legacy2017_EBEB,mass_dielectron_Legacy2017_EBEE,mass_dielectron_Legacy2018_EBEB,mass_dielectron_Legacy2018_EBEE,mass_dimuon_Moriond2017_BB,mass_dimuon_Moriond2017_BE,mass_dimuon_Legacy2017_BB,mass_dimuon_Legacy2017_BE,mass_dimuon_Legacy2018_BB,mass_dimuon_Legacy2018_BE,CMS_channel,weight:_weight_] = 691192 entries (222225 weighted)

Checking data_obs Size Growth

To inspect the growth of data_obs, I used the following script:

import ROOT
import sys


ws_list = ["workspace_VV.root", "workspace_tt.root", "workspace_ll.root", "workspace_combined.root"]

for ws in ws_list:
    f=ROOT.TFile(ws,"READ")
    w=f.Get("w")
    print("checking {}".format(ws))
    for obj in w.allData():
        print("---> {} is {}, numEntries: {}, getSize of each entry {}, assume 8 bytes, total size is {}*{}={} MB".format(obj.GetName(), obj.ClassName(), obj.numEntries(), obj.get().getSize(), obj.numEntries(), obj.get().getSize(),obj.numEntries()* obj.get().getSize()*8/1024.0/1024.0 ))

Observed Output

Singularity> python print.py
checking workspace_VV.root
---> data_obs is RooDataSet, numEntries: 688000, getSize of each entry 4, assume 8 bytes, total size is 688000*4*8/1024.0/1024.0=20.99609375 MB
checking workspace_tt.root
---> data_obs is RooDataSet, numEntries: 1980, getSize of each entry 5, assume 8 bytes, total size is 1980*5*8/1024.0/1024.0=0.0755310058594 MB
checking workspace_ll.root
---> data_obs is RooDataSet, numEntries: 1212, getSize of each entry 14, assume 8 bytes, total size is 1212*14*8/1024.0/1024.0=0.129455566406 MB
checking workspace_combined.root
---> data_obs is RooDataSet, numEntries: 691192, getSize of each entry 21, assume 8 bytes, total size is 691192*21*8/1024.0/1024.0=110.740905762 MB

Issue Summary

Instead of growing linearly, data_obs expands significantly during the combination, likely due to the introduction of additional observable dimensions. This rapid increase ultimately exceeds the ROOT 1 GB limit per object, causing the error.

Besides aligning the name of fitting observable in the workspace, I wonder would you have any recommendations on how to handle this?

Best regards,
Yihui

posts.name: Nicholas Wardle
posts.text:
Hi Yihui,
Are your datacards using TH1 histograms as inputs?
One thing you could try is to avoid the padding when using TH1s by adding the command --X-no-optimize-bins in the text2workspace step.
Best,
Nick
posts.cooked_html:
Hi Yihui,

Are your datacards using TH1 histograms as inputs?

One thing you could try is to avoid the padding when using TH1s by adding the command --X-no-optimize-bins in the text2workspace step.

Best,
Nick

posts.name: Yihui Lai
posts.text:
Hi Nick,
We have about 15 analyses in the combination. More than half of the datacards are not using TH1 histograms, they directly use RooDataSet.
Best,
Yihui
posts.cooked_html:
Hi Nick,

We have about 15 analyses in the combination. More than half of the datacards are not using TH1 histograms, they directly use RooDataSet.

Best,
Yihui

posts.name: Jonathon Mark Langford
posts.text:
Hi Yihui,
We had the same problem when trying to add the offshell analysis regions into the Run 2 Higgs combination, as they have a large number of bins. By default, all channels will share the CMS_th1x variable unless explicitly stated, which grows in dimension with every channel added.
We applied this fix [1]. Analysis regions with bins over some threshold (default 1000 bins) will automatically get a new 1D variable. It’s more memory intensive to have a separate variable for every analysis region, so there’s some compromise. You can tune this threshold according to your setup.
We should probably think of a better fix moving forward which means variables for each region are treated optimally, but this worked for us.
Cheers,
Jon
[1] - https://github.com/cms-analysis/HiggsAnalysis-CombinedLimit/commit/1b6dd8bfb69be7fc8e54305aa8c2384bd853af8c
posts.cooked_html:
Hi Yihui,

We had the same problem when trying to add the offshell analysis regions into the Run 2 Higgs combination, as they have a large number of bins. By default, all channels will share the CMS_th1x variable unless explicitly stated, which grows in dimension with every channel added.

We applied this fix [1]. Analysis regions with bins over some threshold (default 1000 bins) will automatically get a new 1D variable. It’s more memory intensive to have a separate variable for every analysis region, so there’s some compromise. You can tune this threshold according to your setup.

We should probably think of a better fix moving forward which means variables for each region are treated optimally, but this worked for us.

Cheers,
Jon

[1] - https://github.com/cms-analysis/HiggsAnalysis-CombinedLimit/commit/1b6dd8bfb69be7fc8e54305aa8c2384bd853af8c

posts.name: Nicholas Wardle
posts.text:
Hi,
Yes but the point is that the default behaviour will be to pad the datasets for the ones that do use TH1s. Did you try what I suggested?
Nick

posts.name: Yihui Lai
posts.text:
Hi Jon,
Thanks for sharing the fix! But I am not sure if this is the same issue. From my understanding, channel will share CMS_th1x when using TH1. In the case I shared, most analyses use workspace and the variable is explicitly defined, for example: MJ1,MJ2,MJJ,jetmass_LOW_default.
So the padding that Nick mentioned is the issue I am trying to fix. I want to avoid that when having different variables that explicitly defined in workspace.
Best,
Yihui
posts.cooked_html:
Hi Jon,

Thanks for sharing the fix! But I am not sure if this is the same issue. From my understanding, channel will share CMS_th1x when using TH1. In the case I shared, most analyses use workspace and the variable is explicitly defined, for example: MJ1,MJ2,MJJ,jetmass_LOW_default.
So the padding that Nick mentioned is the issue I am trying to fix. I want to avoid that when having different variables that explicitly defined in workspace.

Best,
Yihui

posts.name: Yihui Lai
posts.text:
Hi Nick,
Not sure If I am using the command correctly. Somehow the size gets larger when I add --X-no-optimize-bins.
Without the command:
---> data_obs is RooDataSet, numEntries: 688000, getSize of each entry 4, assume 8 bytes, total size is 688000*4*8/1024.0/1024.0=20.99609375 MB
checking workspace_tt.root
---> data_obs is RooDataSet, numEntries: 408, getSize of each entry 2, assume 8 bytes, total size is 408*2*8/1024.0/1024.0=0.0062255859375 MB
checking workspace_ll.root
---> data_obs is RooDataSet, numEntries: 1212, getSize of each entry 14, assume 8 bytes, total size is 1212*14*8/1024.0/1024.0=0.129455566406 MB
checking workspace_combined.root
---> data_obs is RooDataSet, numEntries: 689620, getSize of each entry 18, assume 8 bytes, total size is 689620*18*8/1024.0/1024.0=94.7048950195 MB

With the command:
combineCards.py input/shapes_DY_Vp_VV_VH_jjjj/datacard_DY_combined_3000.txt input/shapes_DY_Zp_tt_1l/datacards_DY_Zp_tt_3000.txt input/shapes_DY_Zp_ll/datacard_DY_Zp_ll_3000.txt > combined.txt
text2workspace.py input/shapes_DY_Vp_VV_VH_jjjj/datacard_DY_combined_3000.txt --out workspace_VV.root --for-fits --no-wrappers --optimize-simpdf-constraints cms --X-pack-asympows --X-optimizeMHDependency=fixed --optimize-simpdf-constraints=cms --use-histsum  --use-histsum --mass 125.38 --X-no-optimize-bins
text2workspace.py input/shapes_DY_Zp_tt_1l/datacards_DY_Zp_tt_3000.txt --out workspace_tt.root --for-fits --no-wrappers --optimize-simpdf-constraints cms --X-pack-asympows --X-optimizeMHDependency=fixed --optimize-simpdf-constraints=cms --use-histsum  --use-histsum --mass 125.38 --X-no-optimize-bins
text2workspace.py input/shapes_DY_Zp_ll/datacard_DY_Zp_ll_3000.txt --out workspace_ll.root --for-fits --no-wrappers --optimize-simpdf-constraints cms --X-pack-asympows --X-optimizeMHDependency=fixed --optimize-simpdf-constraints=cms --use-histsum  --use-histsum --mass 125.38 --X-no-optimize-bins

checking workspace_VV.root
---> data_obs is RooDataSet, numEntries: 688000, getSize of each entry 4, assume 8 bytes, total size is 688000*4*8/1024.0/1024.0=20.99609375 MB
checking workspace_tt.root
---> data_obs is RooDataSet, numEntries: 363, getSize of each entry 13, assume 8 bytes, total size is 363*13*8/1024.0/1024.0=0.036003112793 MB
checking workspace_ll.root
---> data_obs is RooDataSet, numEntries: 1212, getSize of each entry 14, assume 8 bytes, total size is 1212*14*8/1024.0/1024.0=0.129455566406 MB
checking workspace_combined.root
---> data_obs is RooDataSet, numEntries: 689575, getSize of each entry 29, assume 8 bytes, total size is 689575*29*8/1024.0/1024.0=152.570152283 MB

Best,
Yihui
